findings.title: Post-expiry stakes are miscounted as distributable rewards, creating undercollateralized sHATE

best_agent_match.title: Post-expiry stakes are counted as both backing and next-epoch rewards

findings.title: If staking is multiple epochs behind, the fake reward can be realized immediately

best_agent_match.title: If the contract is multiple epochs behind, the poisoned reward can be realized immediately

findings.title: Raw ERC20 interactions assume strict no-fee, full-return token semantics

best_agent_match.title: Unchecked ERC20 transfer return values in stake()

rejected_candidates.title: Missing reentrancy protection on stake() and unstake()
rejected_candidates.reason: No exploitable state machine beyond token movements was identified; the scenario depends on malicious or highly non-standard token behavior and is better captured by the ERC20-semantics finding rather than as a separate reentrancy issue.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection in stake() and unstake()
rejected_candidates.reason: These functions exchange fixed nominal amounts against the staking contract, not an AMM price curve. The suggested sandwich/front-run path is unsupported by the code.

rejected_candidates.title: Unvalidated distributor address in setDistributor()
rejected_candidates.reason: `setDistributor()` is an `onlyOwner` configuration hook. A malicious or broken owner-selected distributor is a trust/administration assumption, not a permissionless protocol vulnerability shown by this code.

rejected_candidates.title: Race condition in unstake() balance check
rejected_candidates.reason: If the final balance check fails, the entire transaction reverts, including the preceding `sHATE.transferFrom()`. The proposed path does not cause users to lose funds.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero address validation in setDistributor()
rejected_candidates.reason: `rebase()` explicitly skips distributor interaction when `address(distributor) == address(0)`, so setting zero does not brick staking.

rejected_candidates.title: Timestamp manipulation in epoch timing
rejected_candidates.reason: This is only the usual minor `block.timestamp` drift and does not produce realistic protocol-level harm here.
